evidences.title: 2026 Triton Jeju: Information & Tournament Schedule
evidences.content: From March 14 through April 1, 2026, Les A Casino at Jeju Shinhwa World will host what Triton is calling its biggest flagship event to date: 20 tournaments spanning No-Limit Hold'em, Pot-Limit Omaha, and Short Deck, with buy-ins ranging from $20,000 to $150,000. The numbers tell the story of a tour that has consistently redefined what "high stakes" means: Triton's 2025 Jeju festival alone generated nearly $149 million in combined prize pools, and its $100K NLH Main Event became the largest six-figure buy-in tournament in poker history. evidences.title: Triton One Jeju 2026 | March 5 - 15 | Les A Casino
evidences.content: Triton One Jeju 2026 offers more than 10 key tournaments during the 11-day festival. In addition to classic Texas Hold’em, the program naturally includes Pot Limit Omaha, famous for its high-stakes action, and Short Deck (6+), which is extremely popular in Asia. The festival provides an excellent opportunity for up-and-coming pros to test their skills against legends. Check the full schedule at our page. ### Satellites and Qualification Securing a seat at a Triton table has never been more attainable, with various qualification paths available for ambitious players. The organizers are committed to providing opportunities for players to win their way into the biggest events of the series. evidences.title: Triton Poker Series: Schedule, High Stakes Buy-ins & History
evidences.content: Reviewed byCsaba Szirják The Triton Poker Series stands alone at the pinnacle of the poker world, offering the most exclusive Super High Roller tournaments on the planet. Founded to serve a niche of affluent businesspeople and elite professionals, this tour features the highest stakes in history and unparalleled luxury. If you are looking for seven-figure buy-ins, innovative formats like Short Deck, and a field comprised of the world’s wealthiest enthusiasts and sharpest pros, Triton is the gold standard. ## At a Glance Founded: 2016 Region: Asia, Europe Currency: USD ($) Main Venues: + Salle des Étoiles (Monte-Carlo) + Merit Royal Diamond Hotel ( North Cyprus ) + LES A Casino ( Jeju ) + JW Marriott Grosvenor House ( London ) ## History & Prestige [...] Triton SHRS Jeju II S5Triton SHRS Jeju II S5 ![South Korea [KOR]](/assets/images/flags/4x3/kr.svg "South Korea [KOR]")Jeju, LES A Casino](/events-calendar/triton/2026/super-high-roller-series-jeju-ii-s5)[Triton SHRS Montenegro S5 2026 Triton SHRS Montenegro S5 2026Triton SHRS Montenegro S5 2026 ![Montenegro [MNE]](/assets/images/flags/4x3/me.svg "Montenegro [MNE]")Sveti Stefan, Maestral Resort & Casino Montenegro 101.3MUSD 16(34)...

evidences.title: Final Table ICM: How to Play Every Stack – GTO LAB
evidences.content: The core lesson from Ben Heath‘s Triton $100K Jeju analysis: stack-off thresholds rise sharply under ICM from multiple covering stacks. A UTG9 opening range at that final table excluded even ATo as a defend against a larger open, because the combinationf a very tight opener and two large covering stacks behind made marginal calls unprofitable at every equity percentage you might assume is safe. Over-tightening from early position. Waiting for premiums that never come while the blinds eat the stack. The correct play is to find accumulation spots before desperation forces a 5BB shove-anything situation. [...] Nick Petrangelo on playing the big stack at final tables, Tournament Savagery | GTO LAB Attack medium stacks, not short stacks. Short stacks who are desperate will call with wide ranges and race you. Mid stacks protecting their tournament life are far more likely to fold equity to pressure. Tighten into covered big blinds, not widen. Ben Heath‘s analysis of the Triton $100K Jeju final table shows a UTG range that excluded even 88 in certain configurations, because the combination of tight BB defense and multiple covering stacks behind made marginal hands unprofitable despite...
